talk_metadata.session_title: AACR James S. Ewing–Thelma B. Dunn Award for Outstanding Achievement in Pathology
talk_metadata.talk_title: Quantitative Pathology: From Fluorescent Imaging to Selective Biomarkers for ADC Choice
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "If the patient's going to get trastuzumab deruxtecan, they have to have at least a zero plus or originally a one plus, not a zero in order to get that."
Speaker said: "We are still in the process of checking that. I'll tell you, there are at least six trials going on now, two of which or three of which are prospective."
Speaker said: "I think we need a fourth category now that I'm calling a selective biomarker."
Speaker said: "We are negotiating with three different companies who might commercialize it. Because if it can't get commercialized, it will never get beyond my little lab."

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Chromogen intensity varies three‑fold between labs and is affected by staining day‑to‑day variability, preventing reliable quantitative discrimination of HER2 low vs. zero
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Pathologist number one disqualified 44% of patients for T‑DXd, while pathologist number 18 disqualified only 15%, showing patient eligibility depends on reader rather than biology

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that AI segmentation can misclassify lymphocytes as tumor cells ("half of the cells that are lymphocytes are considered to be tumor cells") yet concluded that quantitative AI will soon replace subjective reading without acknowledging this misclassification rate

key_data_gaps: No overall survival data presented for the Troplex assay cohorts
Limited analysis of intrinsic molecular subtypes

crispro_opportunity.description: Lack of a validated quantitative cutoff for HER2‑low ADC eligibility creates a niche for CrisPRO to provide a robust, standardized fluorescence‑based HER2 quantification platform
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "We have two clinical trials ... looking for a cutoff. In preliminary data, it looks like there's not one. Occasionally you see a responder even when they have almost no HER2 at all."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can generate absolute attomole per mm² values with <10% CV and apply machine‑learned threshold optimization across multi‑institutional datasets to define reproducible HER2‑low cutoffs

crispro_opportunity.description: High Trop‑2 expression appears to diminish response to HER2‑targeted ADCs, suggesting a combinatorial resistance mechanism that CrisPRO could model and predict
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "If patients have high Trop‑2 in that same cohort that are given T‑DXd, you do poorly. ... probably you shouldn't give them T‑DXd."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Integrate quantitative HER2 and Trop‑2 data to predict antagonistic biomarker patterns and recommend alternative ADCs (e.g., SG) via a decision‑support algorithm

crispro_opportunity.description: Current ADC trials enroll patients based on binary IHC scores; a trial using CrisPRO quantitative thresholds could reduce enrollment heterogeneity and improve signal detection
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "We are looking at six trials ... two or three are prospective, but we still don't have a clear cutoff."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Provide a validated quantitative assay for trial eligibility criteria, enabling stratified randomization and adaptive cutoff refinement during the study

cited_competitors.name: New York State Department of Health
cited_competitors.context: Regulatory approval of the Troplex assay

cited_competitors.name: Translational Breast Cancer Research Consortium (TBCRC)
cited_competitors.context: Running the QUANTIFY‑HER trial to define HER2‑low cutoffs

unresolved_questions: What is the optimal quantitative HER2 cutoff that predicts response to trastuzumab deruxtecan?
How do intrinsic molecular subtypes (e.g., basal vs. luminal) influence the HER2/Trop‑2 quantitative relationship?
Can pre‑analytical variables be fully eliminated to ensure cross‑site reproducibility of fluorescence‑based quantification?

assets_to_track: HER2‑low ADC clinical trial data (T‑DXd, SG)
companies_to_monitor: Companies negotiating commercialization of the Troplex assay
